title: add buildah login/logout & fix authfile path
body:
Fix the bug that buildah reads /var/run/containers.. as authentication file.(in readme files it should be /run/containers/uid).

Add ` [buildah login`](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1329#issuecomment-470949534) , `biuldah logout` to close #1329, enable to use buildah login before running buildah bud for multi-stage bud and avoid using --creds flag.

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: OK, probably late night brain malfunction, but why do we need `buildah login`?  Can't we just use `podman login`?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 5c51a20) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Why would I require buildah or podman to do login.  You should be able to do the full workflow with either project, and not require one or the other.
@rhatdan it's mostly the duplication of code and man pages that gets me.  Ideally, I'd love to be able to install buildah or podman and have the login/logout code be included from one common place.
Same argument could be made for pull/push/commit/mount/umount ...
If we're doing `buildah login` we need to do `buildah logout` too.

Am I messing something up?  I think we're missing something, but gotta run and can't search for it atm.

```
# buildah login quay.io
unknown command "login" for "buildah"
```
Yes I agree we need buildah logout.  Also we need tests.
Yes, I'll add `logout` and tests in this patch

Could you also fix up.
buildah/demos/README.md, 
buildah/demos/buildah-scratch-demo.sh
```
./docs/buildah-pull.md:  An image in a registry implementing the "Docker Registry HTTP API V2". By default, uses the authorization state in `$XDG\_RUNTIME\_DIR/containers/auth.json`, which is set using `(podman login)`. If the authorization state is not found there, `$HOME/.docker/config.json` is checked, which is set using `(docker login)`.
./docs/buildah-pull.md:Path of the authentication file. Default is ${XDG\_RUNTIME\_DIR}/containers/auth.json, which is set using `podman login`.
```
